description: Evaluation cases for the legal skill (review-contract and meeting-briefing). Each case defines a user prompt, which sub-skill should trigger, and what the output must contain.

expected_behaviors: Recognizes explicit slash-command invocation
Asks user for contract text if none is provided
Does not produce a generic response

cases.prompt: Review this contract for me: [NDA text pasted below]
expected_behaviors: Triggers on 'review this contract' phrase
Identifies the document as an NDA
Produces structured review with risk flags

cases.prompt: What are the red flags in this service agreement?
expected_behaviors: Triggers on 'red flags in this' phrase
Requests contract text if not provided
Produces red/yellow/green flagged output when text is provided
Does NOT invoke legal:review-contract
Answers the general question directly

cases.prompt:
Review this contract:

SERVICE AGREEMENT
This agreement is between ACME Corp (Client) and Dev LLC (Provider). Provider will deliver software services. Client grants Provider unlimited rights to all work product created. There is no liability cap. Payment is due within 90 days. This agreement auto-renews annually unless cancelled 1 day before renewal.
expected_behaviors: Flags 'unlimited rights to all work product' as a red flag (IP ownership issue)
Flags 'no liability cap' as a red flag
Flags '90-day payment terms' as a caution or red flag
Flags '1-day auto-renewal cancellation window' as a red flag
Provides specific recommended replacement language for at least 2 red flags
Includes a risk summary table or structured overview
Notes missing clauses (e.g., confidentiality, governing law, termination for cause)

cases.prompt: Check this NDA: Both parties agree to keep all information confidential forever. Neither party may work with any competitor of the other party for 5 years after the agreement ends. Disputes will be resolved by arbitration in a jurisdiction to be determined later.
expected_behaviors: Flags perpetual confidentiality as potentially overreaching (caution)
Flags 5-year non-compete as a significant red flag (scope and duration)
Flags 'jurisdiction to be determined later' as a red flag
Recommends specific time limits for confidentiality (e.g., 3-5 years)
Recommends specific arbitration venue and rules
Notes NDA should include a definition of 'confidential information'
Does NOT produce a fake contract review
Asks the user to provide contract text or a file path
Explains what formats are accepted (pasted text, file path)
Recognizes explicit slash-command invocation
Asks the 4 context-gathering questions
Does not produce a generic response

cases.prompt: I have a meeting tomorrow with our SaaS vendor about renewing our contract. Help me prepare.
expected_behaviors: Triggers on 'help me prepare' for a meeting phrase
Identifies this as a vendor renewal negotiation
Asks for additional context or proceeds with what's provided
Produces a briefing with talking points and objectives

cases.prompt: Prepare talking points for my negotiation with a new supplier
expected_behaviors: Triggers on 'prepare talking points for'
Identifies this as a supplier negotiation
Produces structured talking points with positions and responses

cases.prompt: What should I bring to a meeting?
expected_behaviors: Does NOT invoke legal:meeting-briefing
Answers the general question directly

cases.prompt: Brief me for my meeting: We're negotiating a software development contract with Acme Corp. They want unlimited IP rights and we want to retain ownership of our reusable components. They are pushing for a fixed price of $50k but our estimate is $80k. Meeting is Monday.
expected_behaviors: Identifies IP ownership and pricing as the two key issues
Provides a position table with Your Position vs Their Position
Includes specific talking points for IP ownership (background vs foreground IP argument)
Includes specific talking points for pricing (scope justification, risk allocation)
Provides at least 2 anticipated objections with response scripts
Includes a walk-away condition
Includes a quick-reference card

cases.prompt: Help me prepare for a dispute meeting with a client who says we delivered late and is threatening to withhold final payment of $25k.
expected_behaviors: Identifies this as a dispute / conflict meeting type
Asks for or notes the need for timeline of events
Frames the situation summary around the payment dispute
Provides talking points around delivery evidence and contract terms
Includes risk assessment (likelihood of escalation, legal exposure)
Recommends post-meeting follow-up actions (written record, legal consultation)
Asks the 4 gathering questions: who, what, goal, background
Does NOT invent a fake meeting scenario
Explains it will generate the briefing once context is provided
